title: Myrcia decrescens
intro: Myrcia decrescens, commonly known as the "Brazilian guava," is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia decrescens include flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin, as well as phenolic acids like ferulic acid and caffeic acid. The most common herbal preparations made with Myrcia decrescens include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
